content.content:
{"body":"* **Suggested term label:**

Clathrin- and Caveolae-Independent Endocytosis
 
* **Definition (free text)**

It is an energy-dependent uptake mechanism that internalizes cargo via small, non-coated vesicles (~40–80 nm) without relying on clathrin or caveolin. These mechanisms often rely on lipid microdomains (lipid rafts) enriched in cholesterol and sphingolipids to mediate membrane invagination and cargo internalization. Can be dynamin-dependent or dynamin-independent. 
 
* **Reference, in format PMID:#######**
[31976201](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31976201/)
[15668298](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15668298/)
[30774931](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30774931/)
16046009
12621426

* **Parent term**
GO:0006897 endocytosis
","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws672D2Z","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"+[Term]
+id: GO:0160294
+name: clathrin- and caveolae-independent endocytosis
+namespace: biological_process
+def: "An endocytosis process that internalizes cargo via small, non-coated vesicles (~40-80 nm) without relying on clathrin or caveolin and can be dynamin-dependent or dynamin-independent." [PMID:15668298, PMID:31976201]
+is_a: GO:0006897 ! endocytosis
+property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667" xsd:anyURI
+created_by: rynl
+creation_date: 2025-08-04T16:38:59Z
","createdAt":"2025-08-04T16:46:51Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-3151510937","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws67_nPX","author":{"login":"lbreuza"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Not sure it is relevant to create this term as I do not see a cell machinery involved in this process. Would it be a parent term for already existing child terms of endocytosis like pinocytosis?","createdAt":"2025-08-05T08:04:51Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-3154015191","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws68AVDQ","author":{"login":"mariacastillo982"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"Thank you for your feedback.

The motivation for proposing the term “clathrin- and caveolae-independent endocytosis” is to reflect a distinct route of endocytosis (of type pinocytosis) that is mechanistically different from clathrin-mediated and caveolin-mediated pathways. While it is true that some of the molecular machinery involved remains incompletely defined, studies (e.g., PMID:15668298, PMID:31976201) characterize this process as involving small, non-coated vesicles and that is independent of both clathrin and caveolin. 
It may provide a useful organizational node for terms like the GEEC pathway, or Arf6-mediated endocytosis, which are often classified under this umbrella in the literature but currently lack a common parent that explicitly excludes clathrin and caveolin dependence.","createdAt":"2025-08-05T09:03:04Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-3154202832","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws68C68-","author":{"login":"lbreuza"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Thanks Maria,
if you see it as a a useful organizational node maybe one should assign the different child of endocytosis to one of these organizational nodes? Are you going to curate ARF6 to this term?
","createdAt":"2025-08-05T11:47:50Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-3154882366","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABFVFsDQ","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"There are a few concerns with this term.

This review presents 2 pathways as Clathrin- and Caveolae-Independent Endocytosis
and reproduces a figure from a 2011 publication but I don;t see any direct evidence that these pathways exist (if they do the pathways should be instantiated,  not a  x and y  independnet term

The review also focuses on cell penetrating pepetides, biut these do not appear to be a vesicle-mediated process

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15668298/
because this is viral entry, it might not *require*  host machinery (or the actin cytoskeleton alone is sufficent to form endocytic vesicles). This can occur in yeast if  clathrin is deleted but actin is overexpressed. But presumably with some  sory of external force from the virus it si likly to be possible to force invagination.
Any upregulation of actin is likely to compensate for absence of   coat components, so care needs to be taken when experiemtns are performed in cancer cell lines etc.

I recommend: 
i) obsoletion UNLESS there are specific genes to be annotated to this pathway 9currently there are no annotations)

ii) If there are Clathrin- and Caveolae-Independent pathways the terms are added  with explicit dependencies rather than independencies (ensuring these are WT processes, and not pathogeneic  situations or  cancer/disease cell lines)

","createdAt":"2026-06-08T19:23:50Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4652624909","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGsXc9A","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Given that there are no annotations with GO:0160294 clathrin- and caveolae-independent endocytosis AFAIK, I think we should obsolete this term. @mariacastillo982 please comment if you have concerns. Thanks.","createdAt":"2026-06-18T16:39:24Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4744142068","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGtOhIw","author":{"login":"mariacastillo982"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"Thanks for the review. I understand the concern with an absence-based label, since terms defined only by what they lack can group heterogeneous mechanisms. I'd like to propose retaining the concept but reframing it around its positive, identifiable features, and I want to show the concrete annotation needed to drive the request.

Proposed revised definition:
"An endocytosis mechanism in which cargo is internalized via small, non-coated vesicles (~40–80 nm) whose formation depends on cholesterol- and sphingolipid-enriched membrane microdomains, and which may be either dynamin-dependent or dynamin-independent."

This removes the clathrin/caveolae-independent negative framing and defines the term by its mechanism. It would sit under GO:0006897 (endocytosis), and several well-characterized sub-mechanisms (CLIC/GEEC, Arf6-associated CIE, FEME, flotillin-dependent uptake) could be organized beneath or alongside it.

There is clear experimental literature and a real annotation backlog this term would resolve:

- CLIC/GEEC: GRAF1 (ARHGAP26) and CDC42 mark and are required for the pathway — PMID:19036340 (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cub.2008.10.044); CLIC/GEEC also used for AAV2 entry, sensitive to CDC42/ARF1/GRAF1 — PMID:22177561 (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chom.2011.10.014)
- Arf6-associated CIE cargo: CD44, CD55, CD98/SLC3A2, CD147/BSG, GLUT1/SLC2A1, ICAM1 — PMID:19302270 (https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1600-0854.2009.00894.x); endosomal sorting via HOOK1 and RAB22A — PMID:24284901 (https://doi.org/10.4161/bioa.26638)
- FEME: SH3GL2 (endophilin), RAPH1 (lamellipodin), SHIP1/2 — PMID:25517094 (https://doi.org/10.1038/nature14067)
- Flotillin-dependent uptake: FLOT1 — PMID:24042857 (https://doi.org/10.3892/ijmm.2013.1492)

These provide experimental support for gene-to-term annotations that are currently lacking. Would a positively-framed term along these lines address the obsolescence concern? Happy to adjust the label and definition to fit editorial conventions, and to split sub-mechanisms into separate children if preferred.","createdAt":"2026-06-18T18:35:48Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4745044259","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGvQN3Q","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I went back through all the references cited above to test a specific question: do they support a single, coherent, **positively-defined** pathway, or do they describe several distinct mechanisms? All eight PMIDs are real and accurately described, but read together they point to the latter.

**The references describe at least four mechanistically distinct routes that disagree on every axis used to individuate an endocytic pathway:**

| Pathway | Master regulator | Dynamin | Marker | Carrier |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CLIC/GEEC (PMID:19036340, PMID:22177561) | CDC42 **activated** | **independent** | GRAF1 | tubular/ring |
| FEME (PMID:25517094) | CDC42 **inhibited** | **dependent** | endophilin (SH3GL2) | leading-edge tubules |
| Flotillin-dependent (PMID:24042857) | — | variable | FLOT1 | punctate raft |
| Arf6-associated CIE (PMID:19302270) | ARF6 | variable | Arf6 | recycling tubules |
| SV40 route (PMID:15668298) | excludes clathrin/dynII/**ARF6** | independent | — | small vesicles |

Decisive contradictions:

- **CDC42 plays opposite roles**: CLIC/GEEC is CDC42-*activated*; FEME is activated by CDC42 *inhibition* (PMID:25517094).
- **Dynamin requirement is opposite** between CLIC/GEEC (independent) and FEME (dependent).
- **Markers are mutually exclusive**: Lundmark (PMID:19036340) shows CLIC/GEEC carriers are *devoid of flotillin-1*, so the flotillin and CLIC/GEEC routes are physically different carriers.
- The two references currently in the GO:0160294 definition disagree with each other: the SV40 paper (PMID:15668298) explicitly excludes ARF6, while the Arf6-CIE paper (PMID:19302270) defines its pathway *by* ARF6.
- The foundational CLIC/GEEC paper itself opens by calling clathrin-independent endocytosis "an umbrella term for a variety of endocytic pathways" (PMID:19036340) — i.e., a category, not a pathway.

**On the proposed positive reframing** (small non-coated ~40–80 nm vesicles forming in cholesterol-/sphingolipid-enriched microdomains, dynamin-dependent or -independent): unfortunately this doesn't unify the set. The lipid-microdomain criterion fits the flotillin and SV40 routes but not FEME (endophilin/curvature/PI(3,4)P2-driven) and is not what gates CLIC/GEEC biogenesis (CDC42/actin/GRAF1-driven; GPI-anchored cargo are raft-associated, but carrier formation is not raft-dependent). The "40–80 nm round vesicle" morphology also fails for CLIC/GEEC, whose carriers are tubular/ring-shaped, and "dynamin-dependent or -independent" concedes the heterogeneity. As written it is an under-constrained description that several different processes each partially satisfy, which would cause mis-grouping under the reasoner.

A separate point on the proposed annotation set: the machinery/marker genes (GRAF1/ARHGAP26, endophilin/SH3GL2, lamellipodin/RAPH1, FLOT1) are clean subjects for pathway annotation, but the Arf6-CIE list (CD44, CD55, CD98/SLC3A2, CD147/BSG, GLUT1/SLC2A1, ICAM1) are **cargo** — passengers through the pathway rather than components of it.

**Recommendation**

1. Obsolete GO:0160294. It is negatively defined (the original concern), has no annotations, and its own definitional references describe different pathways.
2. Where there is curator demand, capture the annotation need with **specific, positively-defined children of GO:0006897 (endocytosis)**, each defined by its own machinery rather than by a shared absence:
   - **CLIC/GEEC pathway** — GRAF1 (ARHGAP26), CDC42 (PMID:19036340, PMID:22177561)
   - **FEME / fast endophilin-mediated endocytosis** — endophilin (SH3GL2), lamellipodin (RAPH1) (PMID:25517094)
   - Flotillin-dependent and Arf6-dependent CIE are more weakly individuated in the current literature; I'd treat those case-by-case if specific annotations require them.

This satisfies the underlying request — giving GRAF1, endophilin, etc. a home — without a single umbrella node that the evidence doesn't support. Happy to draft the CLIC/GEEC and FEME terms if there's agreement on this direction.
","createdAt":"2026-06-19T00:16:12Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4747169245","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGxlYvw","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Great, I agree the way forward is to instantiate the specific pathways.

Note that some of these,  may class as endocytosis, but not vesicle-mediated transport.
I think if strictly defined v-m-t is defined by budding and fusion,  driven by specific coat protein complexes that curve the membrane.

CLIC/GEEC pathway is not classiclayy defined as v-m-t.

In fact, at the moment endocytosis is wholly under vesicle-mediated transport, which is incorrect.","createdAt":"2026-06-19T07:56:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4749613247","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABHJWJsw","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please remove the asserted relationship GO:0006897 endocytosis is_a GO:0016192 vesicle-mediated transport. Do not close this ticket.","createdAt":"2026-06-23T00:55:37Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4774529459","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABHJWPjg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27994586715)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-23T00:55:58Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667#issuecomment-4774530958","viewerDidAuthor":true}],"title":"NTR: Clathrin- and Caveolae-Independent Endocytosis (reopened)"}

   - **FEME / fast endophilin-mediated endocytosis** — endophilin (SH3GL2), lamellipodin (RAPH1) (PMID:25517094)

This satisfies the underlying request — giving GRAF1, endophilin, etc. a home — without a single umbrella node that the evidence doesn't support. Happy to draft the CLIC/GEEC and FEME terms if there's agreement on this direction.

input.command:
git add src/ontology/go-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Remove asserted is_a GO:0016192 vesicle-mediated transport from GO:0006897 endocytosis

Per discussion in issue #30667: endocytosis as a category includes routes
(e.g. CLIC/GEEC) that are not classically vesicle-mediated transport (which
in the strict sense is defined by budding/fusion of coat-driven vesicles).
Asserting endocytosis is_a vesicle-mediated transport wholesale is therefore
incorrect. The term retains its other parent is_a GO:0098657 ! import into
cell, so it is not orphaned.

Refs: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30667

---

- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: View workflow run https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27994586715
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Remove asserted is_a vesicle-mediated transport from endocytosis (#30667)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Removes the asserted `is_a: GO:0016192 ! vesicle-mediated transport` from `GO:0006897 endocytosis` per @raymond91125's request on issue #30667, following the discussion summarized by @ValWood:

> Note that some of these may class as endocytosis, but not vesicle-mediated transport. I think if strictly defined v-m-t is defined by budding and fusion, driven by specific coat protein complexes that curve the membrane. CLIC/GEEC pathway is not classically defined as v-m-t. In fact, at the moment endocytosis is wholly under vesicle-mediated transport, which is incorrect.

## Changes

- `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`: drop one line on `GO:0006897 endocytosis`:
  - `- is_a: GO:0016192 ! vesicle-mediated transport`

The term retains `is_a: GO:0098657 ! import into cell`, so it is not orphaned.

Out of scope (left for follow-up in #30667):
- The text definition still opens with "A vesicle-mediated transport process …". Not touched here because the request was scoped to the asserted relationship, but it should probably be revisited as part of the broader restructuring discussed in the issue.
- The `has_part GO:0006900 ! vesicle budding from membrane` relationship is retained. Vesicle budding is itself a sub-process of vesicle-mediated transport, but `has_part` is the right relation for a coated-vesicle endocytosis subtree, not for the parent class — happy to revisit if curators want this rethought.
- Subclasses of endocytosis that ARE genuinely vesicle-mediated transport (e.g. clathrin-mediated endocytosis) are unaffected and would need explicit `is_a GO:0016192` if curators want that captured directly, rather than inheriting it from the parent.

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN — issue read, intent clear (drop one asserted axiom; ticket stays open)
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION — ontology validated cleanly prior to the edit (SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning)
- [N/A] RESEARCH — not needed; the curatorial decision is recorded in the issue
- [N/A] TERM-SEARCH — single known term, no lookups required
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS — no new term, no logical definition added
- [x] EDITS — used `obo-checkout.pl` / edit / `obo-checkin.pl` workflow
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS — `is_a GO:0098657 ! import into cell` retained, no orphaning; other relationships untouched
- [N/A] SPECIALIZED-EDITS (obsoletion / CHEBI / reaction / taxon-constraint)
- [N/A] METADATA — not a new term; no `created_by`/`creation_date` change
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — `robot convert` (syntax), `robot reason -r ELK` (reasoning), and the full SPARQL QC suite all pass; no unsatisfiable classes introduced
- [N/A] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — no new references introduced
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED — only `src/ontology/go-edit.obo` modified

## Test plan

- [x] `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` — passes
- [x] `robot reason -r ELK -i go-edit.obo` — passes, no unsats
- [x] All 16 SPARQL QC rules — PASS, 0 violations

Linked issue: #30667 (kept open per request).

---

- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27994586715)
EOF
)"

gh issue comment 30667 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Done — opened #32231 to remove the asserted `is_a GO:0016192 vesicle-mediated transport` from `GO:0006897 endocytosis`. The term keeps `is_a GO:0098657 ! import into cell` as its parent, so nothing gets orphaned. Issue is left open as requested.

Two things I deliberately did NOT touch, since they weren't in the request — flagging in case you want them addressed in a follow-up:

1. The text definition still opens with "A vesicle-mediated transport process …". Probably wants a rewrite given the structural change.
2. `relationship: has_part GO:0006900 ! vesicle budding from membrane` is retained on the parent. That fits coated-vesicle endocytosis subtypes but is questionable as a property of the parent class given the discussion (CLIC/GEEC etc. wouldn't share it). Worth a separate look.

Subclasses of endocytosis that are genuinely vesicle-mediated transport (e.g. clathrin-mediated endocytosis) will no longer inherit `is_a vesicle-mediated transport` from the parent. If you want any of them to keep that classification, they'd need an explicit `is_a GO:0016192` asserted directly — happy to follow up on a list if you can specify which.

Validation: `robot convert`, `robot reason -r ELK`, and the full 16-rule SPARQL QC suite all pass. No unsatisfiable classes.
